<!-- To ensure we can review your pull request promptly please complete this template entirely. -->

<!-- Please reference the issue number here. You can replace "Fixes" with "Closes" if it makes more sense. -->
Fixes #162

Changes proposed in this pull request:
<!-- Please list all changes/additions here. -->
- Add `jsonl_to_markdown.py` utility script to convert JSONL files to Markdown format
- Script extracts the 'text' field from each line in a JSONL file and saves it as a separate Markdown file
- Include proper error handling for JSON decoding and file operations
- Add example usage and documentation

## Before submitting

<!-- Please complete this checklist BEFORE submitting your PR to speed along the review process. -->
- [x] I've read and followed all steps in the [Making a pull request](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#making-a-pull-request) section of the `CONTRIBUTING` docs.
- [x] I've updated or added relevant docstrings following the syntax described in the [Writing docstrings](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#writing-docstrings) section of the `CONTRIBUTING` docs.
- [x] If this PR fixes a bug, I've added a test that will fail without my fix.
- [x] If this PR adds a new feature, I've added tests that sufficiently cover my new functionality.

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

A toolkit for converting PDFs and other image-based document formats into clean, readable, plain text format. Try the online demo: [https://olmocr.allenai.org/](https://olmocr.allenai.org/) Features: - Convert PDF, PNG, and JPEG based documents into clean Markdown - Support for equations, tables, handwriting, and complex formatting - Automatically removes headers and footers - Convert into text with a natural reading order, even in the presence of figures, multi-column layouts, and insets - Efficient, less than $200 USD per million pages converted - (Based on a 7B parameter VLM, so it requires a GPU) ### News - May 19, 2025 - v0.1.68 - [olmOCR-Bench](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/tree/main/olmocr/bench) launch, scoring 77.4. Launch includes 2 point performance boost in olmOCR pipeline due to bug fixes with prompts. - Mar 17, 2025 - v0.1.60 - Performance improvements due to better temperature selection in sampling. - Feb 25, 2025 - v0.1.58 - Initial public launch and demo. ### Benchmark [**olmOCR-Bench**](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/tree/main/olmocr/bench): We also ship a comprehensive benchmark suite covering over 7,000 test cases across 1,400 documents to help measure performance of OCR systems.
Model AR OSM TA OS HF MC LTT Base Overall Score
Marker v1.6.2 24.3 22.1 69.8 24.3 87.1 71.0

arguments.reason: PR is about fixing/adding jsonl_to_markdown utility; need to inspect existing implementation and entrypoints.
arguments.expected_information: CLI interface, how JSONL lines are parsed, output naming/location, error handling, and any functions to adjust or test.

tool_calls.raw_result:
import json
import os
import sys


# This is a simple script to convert JSONL files to Markdown format.
# It reads each line of the JSONL file, extracts the 'text' field,
# and saves it as a Markdown file with the line number as the filename.
# The script also handles potential JSON decoding errors and prints relevant messages.
def jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir):
    """
    Reads a JSONL file, extracts the 'text' field from each line, and saves it as a Markdown file.

    Args:
        input_file (str): Path to the input JSONL file.
        output_dir (str): Directory to save the Markdown files.
    """
    if not os.path.exists(output_dir):
        os.makedirs(output_dir)

    with open(input_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as file:
        for i, line in enumerate(file):
            try:
                # Parse the JSON line
                data = json.loads(line)
                text_content = data.get("text", "")

                # Save to a Markdown file
                output_file = os.path.join(output_dir, f"line_{i + 1}.md")
                with open(output_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as md_file:
                    md_file.write(text_content)

                print(f"Extracted and saved line {i + 1} to {output_file}")
            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
                print(f"Error decoding JSON on line {i + 1}: {e}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Unexpected error on line {i + 1}: {e}")


# Example usage
# input_jsonl_file = "/path/to/test.jsonl"  # Replace with the actual path to your JSONL file
# output_directory = "/path/to/output_markdown"  # Replace with the desired output directory
# jsonl_to_markdown(input_jsonl_file, output_directory)

# This is the main entrypoint to use the script from the command line.
# It takes two arguments: the input JSONL file and the output directory.
# The script will create the output directory if it does not exist.
if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
        print("Usage: python jsonl_to_markdown.py <input_file> <output_dir>")
        sys.exit(1)

    input_file = sys.argv[1]
    output_dir = sys.argv[2]

    jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` is a small CLI script that converts a JSONL file into multiple Markdown files by extracting the `"text"` field from each JSON object line-by-line.

### Architecture
- Uses a single utility function, `jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)`, that contains all conversion logic (directory creation, file reading, JSON parsing, markdown writing, and error reporting).
- A `__main__` block serves as the command-line entrypoint, validates argument count, and calls `jsonl_to_markdown`.

### Functions & Classes
- `jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)` — Core conversion routine.
  - Ensures `output_dir` exists (`os.path.exists` + `os.makedirs`).
  - Iterates through the JSONL file with `enumerate(file)` to track line numbers (1-based in output naming/messages).
  - For each line:
    - Parses JSON via `json.loads(line)`.
    - Extracts content via `data.get("text", "")` (defaults to empty string if missing).
    - Writes a markdown file named `line_{i + 1}.md` into `output_dir`.
  - Error handling:
    - Catches `json.JSONDecodeError` and prints a line-specific error.
    - Catches generic `Exception` and prints an “Unexpected error” message.
  - Prints a success message for each extracted line/file written.
- `if __name__ == "__main__":` — CLI entrypoint.
  - Requires exactly 2 CLI args beyond the script name (`len(sys.argv) != 3`).
  - On mismatch, prints `Usage: python jsonl_to_markdown.py <input_file> <output_dir>` and exits with code `1`.
  - Otherwise reads `input_file` and `output_dir` from `sys.argv` and calls `jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)`.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **CLI interface/entrypoint:** Defined in the `__main__` block; currently minimal (only positional args, strict count check, basic usage string).
- **JSONL parsing behavior:** One JSON object per line parsed by `json.loads(line)`; only the `"text"` field is used, with silent fallback to `""` if absent.
- **Output naming/location:** Creates one file per input line named `line_{N}.md` in `output_dir`; this is the key behavior to adjust if the PR needs different naming, aggregation, or directory structure.
- **Error handling:** Errors are only printed (no non-zero exit on parse failures, no summary, continues processing). This is likely a focal point for improving robustness/testing (e.g., fail-fast option, logging, counting failures, or propagating exceptions).

import json
import os
import sys


# This is a simple script to convert JSONL files to Markdown format.
# It reads each line of the JSONL file, extracts the 'text' field,
# and saves it as a Markdown file with the line number as the filename.
# The script also handles potential JSON decoding errors and prints relevant messages.
def jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir):
    """
    Reads a JSONL file, extracts the 'text' field from each line, and saves it as a Markdown file.

    Args:
        input_file (str): Path to the input JSONL file.
        output_dir (str): Directory to save the Markdown files.
    """
    if not os.path.exists(output_dir):
        os.makedirs(output_dir)

    with open(input_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as file:
        for i, line in enumerate(file):
            try:
                # Parse the JSON line
                data = json.loads(line)
                text_content = data.get("text", "")

                # Save to a Markdown file
                output_file = os.path.join(output_dir, f"line_{i + 1}.md")
                with open(output_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as md_file:
                    md_file.write(text_content)

                print(f"Extracted and saved line {i + 1} to {output_file}")
            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
                print(f"Error decoding JSON on line {i + 1}: {e}")
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Unexpected error on line {i + 1}: {e}")


# Example usage
# input_jsonl_file = "/path/to/test.jsonl"  # Replace with the actual path to your JSONL file
# output_directory = "/path/to/output_markdown"  # Replace with the desired output directory
# jsonl_to_markdown(input_jsonl_file, output_directory)

# This is the main entrypoint to use the script from the command line.
# It takes two arguments: the input JSONL file and the output directory.
# The script will create the output directory if it does not exist.
if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
        print("Usage: python jsonl_to_markdown.py <input_file> <output_dir>")
        sys.exit(1)

    input_file = sys.argv[1]
    output_dir = sys.argv[2]

    jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`docs/source/index.md` is the Sphinx documentation landing page for the project, defining the top-level navigation structure and what sections appear in the site’s sidebar/toctrees.

### Architecture
The file is organized around two Sphinx `toctree` blocks:
- **Getting started**: includes local documentation pages (`installation`, `overview`).
- **Development**: includes local pages (`CHANGELOG`, `CONTRIBUTING`) plus external links (LICENSE raw URL and GitHub repo).
Both toctrees are marked `:hidden:`, meaning they define navigation but don’t render their entries inline on the index page. The bottom section adds index links via an `eval-rst` block.

### Functions & Classes
No functions, classes, or methods are defined in this file.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This is the primary place to **add a new documentation section/link** so users can discover example usage for the `jsonl_to_markdown` script.
- Since there is currently no “Scripts”, “Utilities”, or “CLI tools” section, you would likely add:

### Architecture
The file is organized around two Sphinx `toctree` blocks:
- **Getting started**: includes local documentation pages (`installation`, `overview`).
- **Development**: includes local pages (`CHANGELOG`, `CONTRIBUTING`) plus external links (LICENSE raw URL and GitHub repo).
Both toctrees are marked `:hidden:`, meaning they define navigation but don’t render their entries inline on the index page. The bottom section adds index links via an `eval-rst` block.

### Functions & Classes
No functions, classes, or methods are defined in this file.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal

# Configuration file for the Sphinx documentation builder.
#
# This file only contains a selection of the most common options. For a full
# list see the documentation:
# https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/configuration.html

import logging
import os
import sys
from datetime import datetime

# -- Path setup --------------------------------------------------------------

# If extensions (or modules to document with autodoc) are in another directory,
# add these directories to sys.path here. If the directory is relative to the
# documentation root, use os.path.abspath to make it absolute, like shown here.
#

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../../"))

from olmocr import VERSION, VERSION_SHORT  # noqa: E402

# -- Project information -----------------------------------------------------

project = "olmocr"
copyright = f"{datetime.today().year}, Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence"
author = "Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence"
version = VERSION_SHORT
release = VERSION


# -- General configuration ---------------------------------------------------

# Add any Sphinx extension module names here, as strings. They can be
# extensions coming with Sphinx (named 'sphinx.ext.*') or your custom
# ones.
extensions = [
    "sphinx.ext.autodoc",
    "sphinx.ext.napoleon",
    "myst_parser",
    "sphinx.ext.intersphinx",
    "sphinx.ext.viewcode",
    "sphinx.ext.doctest",
    "sphinx_copybutton",
    "sphinx_autodoc_typehints",
]

# Tell myst-parser to assign header anchors for h1-h3.
myst_heading_anchors = 3

suppress_warnings = ["myst.header"]

# Add any paths that contain templates here, relative to this directory.
templates_path = ["_templates"]

# List of patterns, relative to source directory, that match files and
# directories to ignore when looking for source files.
# This pattern also affects html_static_path and html_extra_path.
exclude_patterns = ["_build"]

source_suffix = [".rst", ".md"]

intersphinx_mapping = {
    "python": ("https://docs.python.org/3", None),
    # Uncomment these if you use them in your codebase:
    #  "torch": ("https://pytorch.org/docs/stable", None),

}

# By default, sort documented members by type within classes and modules.

# Include default values when documenting parameter types.
typehints_defaults = "comma"


# -- Options for HTML output -------------------------------------------------

# The theme to use for HTML and HTML Help pages.  See the documentation for
# a list of builtin themes.
#
html_theme = "furo"

html_title = f"olmocr v{VERSION}"

# Add any paths that contain custom static files (such as style sheets) here,
# relative to this directory. They are copied after the builtin static files,
# so a file named "default.css" will overwrite the builtin "default.css".
html_static_path = ["_static"]

html_css_files = ["css/custom.css"]

html_favicon = "_static/favicon.ico"

html_theme_options = {
    "footer_icons": [
        {
            "name": "GitHub",
            "url": "https://github.com/allenai/olmocr",
            "html": """
                <svg stroke="currentColor" fill="currentColor" stroke-width="0" viewBox="0 0 16 16">

                </svg>
            """,  # noqa: E501
            "class": "",

# -- Hack to get rid of stupid warnings from sphinx_autodoc_typehints --------


class ShutupSphinxAutodocTypehintsFilter(logging.Filter):
    def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:
        if "Cannot resolve forward reference" in record.msg:
            return False
        return True

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`docs/source/conf.py` configures the Sphinx documentation build for the `olmocr` project, including enabling MyST Markdown support and setting HTML theme/options.

### Architecture
- **Path/setup layer**: Modifies `sys.path` to include the repo root (`../../`) so Sphinx can import `olmocr` and read version metadata.
- **Sphinx configuration layer**: Declares enabled Sphinx extensions (notably `myst_parser`), source file suffixes (`.rst`, `.md`), exclusion patterns, and autodoc/type-hint behavior.
- **HTML output layer**: Configures the `furo` theme, title, static assets, and theme options (footer GitHub icon).
- **Logging noise suppression layer**: Adds a custom `logging.Filter` to suppress specific warnings coming from `sphinx_autodoc_typehints`.

### Functions & Classes
- `ShutupSphinxAutodocTypehintsFilter` — custom `logging.Filter` subclass used to filter out noisy warnings from `sphinx_autodoc_typehints`.
  - `filter` — returns `False` (suppresses the log record) when the message contains `"Cannot resolve forward reference"`, otherwise allows it; important to keep docs builds clean/failures less likely due to warning spam.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **MyST Markdown is enabled** via `extensions = [..., "myst_parser", ...]`.
- **Markdown files are included in the build** via `source_suffix = [".rst", ".md"]`.
- **MyST header behavior**: `myst_heading_anchors = 3` enables automatic anchors for headings `h1`–`h3`, and `suppress_warnings = ["myst.header"]` suppresses certain MyST header warnings (useful to know if adding Markdown that might otherwise warn).
- **Exclusions/patterns**: `exclude_patterns = ["_build"]` is the only exclusion listed—no special exclusions for `scripts/` or other directories are present here; whether “scripts docs” are included depends on what’s in the Sphinx `toctree` and the `docs/source` content, not on additional ignore patterns in this config.
- **Autodoc considerations**: `sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../../"))` and `sphinx.ext.autodoc` indicate API docs can import project code; the logging filter suggests forward references in type hints may exist and would otherwise generate warnings during docs builds.

### File Overview
`docs/source/conf.py` configures the Sphinx documentation build for the `olmocr` project, including enabling MyST Markdown support and setting HTML theme/options.

### Architecture
- **Path/setup layer**: Modifies `sys.path` to include the repo root (`../../`) so Sphinx can import `olmocr` and read version metadata.
- **Sphinx configuration layer**: Declares enabled Sphinx extensions (notably `myst_parser`), source file suffixes (`.rst`, `.md`), exclusion patterns, and autodoc/type-hint behavior.
- **HTML output layer**: Configures the `furo` theme, title, static assets, and theme options (footer GitHub icon).
- **Logging noise suppression layer**: Adds a custom `logging.Filter` to suppress specific warnings coming from `sphinx_autodoc_typehints`.

### Functions & Classes
- `ShutupSphinxAutodocTypehintsFilter` — custom `logging.Filter` subclass used to filter out noisy warnings from `sphinx_autodoc_typehints`.
  - `filter` — returns `False` (suppresses the log record) when the message contains `"Cannot resolve forward reference"`, otherwise allows it; important to keep docs builds clean/failures less likely due to warning spam.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **MyST Markdown is enabled** via `extensions = [..., "myst_parser", ...]`.
- **Markdown files are included in the build** via `source_suffix = [".rst", ".md"]`.
- **MyST header behavior**: `myst_heading_anchors = 3` enables automatic anchors for headings `h1`–`h3`, and `suppress_warnings = ["myst.header"]` suppresses certain MyST header warnings (useful to know if adding Markdown that might otherwise warn).

output.pr_body:
Fixes #162

Changes proposed in this pull request:
- Add `jsonl_to_markdown.py` utility script to convert JSONL files to Markdown format
- Script extracts the 'text' field from each line in a JSONL file and saves it as a separate Markdown file
- Include proper error handling for JSON decoding and file operations
- Add example usage and documentation

Checklist:
- Read contributing docs
- Updated docstrings
- Added tests
output.masca_optimized:
### MASCA (optimized for PR: `fix jsonl_to_markdown` / issue #162)

#### Mission
Ensure the `jsonl_to_markdown` utility works reliably as a supported conversion tool:
- Convert a JSONL file into per-record Markdown files by extracting the `text` field.
- Improve/solidify error handling (JSON decoding failures, missing fields, file I/O issues).
- Provide discoverable documentation and example usage.
- Add tests that lock in expected behavior.

#### App/Module Surfaces involved
- **Script entrypoint**: `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` (currently exists in repo and already implements basic functionality).
- **Docs**: Sphinx (MyST Markdown enabled). User-facing docs live under `docs/source/` and are navigated via `docs/source/index.md` toctrees.
- **Tests**: `tests/` has no existing coverage for this script; new tests need to be introduced.

#### Current Behavior (as-is)
- `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` defines `jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)` and a minimal `__main__` CLI.
- For each input line (JSON object), it writes `output_dir/line_{N}.md` containing `data.get("text", "")`.
- Error handling is print-based:
  - On `json.JSONDecodeError`, prints an error and continues.
  - On other exceptions, prints an “Unexpected error” and continues.
  - No clear exit code semantics or summary of failures.

#### Gaps likely driving issue #162
- “Fix” suggests the existing script may have one or more of:
  - Incorrect CLI usage expectations (strict arg count, no `--help`, etc.).
  - Fragile error handling (swallows errors, continues silently producing empty files when `text` missing, etc.).
  - Unclear output naming or collisions.
  - Missing tests and missing docs discoverability.

#### Acceptance Targets (what to implement/change)
1. **Robust conversion function contract**
   - Decide desired behavior for:
     - Missing `text` field (skip? write empty file? error?).
     - Blank lines.
     - JSON decode failure (skip with counter; optionally fail fast).
   - Ensure output dir creation is reliable and errors are actionable.

2. **CLI improvements (if intended by issue/PR)**
   - Consider using `argparse` for:
     - `input_file`, `output_dir`
     - optional `--text-key` (default `text`)
     - optional `--prefix` or naming scheme
     - optional `--fail-fast` / `--skip-empty`
   - Ensure non-zero exit code when appropriate (e.g., cannot read input file, cannot write output, or all lines failed).

3. **Docs**
   - Add a new doc page under `docs/source/` documenting `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` usage and examples.
   - Link it from `docs/source/index.md` (new “Utilities/Scripts” toctree or add to an existing one).

4. **Tests**
   - Add tests that exercise:
     - happy path with multiple JSONL lines producing multiple `.md` files.
     - invalid JSON line handling.
     - missing `text` field behavior.
     - output directory creation.
   - Prefer calling the function directly (unit test) rather than spawning subprocess, unless CLI semantics are a key requirement.

#### Code hotspots / change points
- `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py::jsonl_to_markdown` (core conversion & error semantics).
- `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` `__main__` block (CLI contract and exit codes).
- New `tests/test_jsonl_to_markdown.py` (or similar).
- New docs page: `docs/source/jsonl_to_markdown.md` (or `docs/source/scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.md` if you add a `scripts/` docs folder).
- `docs/source/index.md` to add the page to navigation.

files_to_modify.reason: This is the existing utility script; PR/issue indicates it needs to be fixed/solidified (conversion behavior, error handling, and possibly CLI improvements).
No existing tests cover this script; PR checklist claims tests were added. Add unit tests to lock in behavior for valid/invalid JSONL lines, missing text field handling, and output file generation.
To make the new/updated utility discoverable in built docs, a new doc page should be linked in the Sphinx toctree.
Add user-facing documentation and example usage for the script (MyST Markdown is enabled).

functions_to_modify.reason: Primary conversion logic; needs any bugfixes plus clarified behavior for missing `text`, invalid JSON, and file I/O errors, consistent with issue #162 and the PR description.
CLI currently only checks positional arg count and prints usage. If issue #162 relates to usability or error behavior, adjust argument parsing and exit code handling here.

output.analysis_summary: Repository already contains `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` implementing JSONL->multiple Markdown files by extracting the `text` field and writing `line_{N}.md`. There are currently no tests referencing this script and no docs page for it in `docs/source/`. To satisfy the PR description (fixing the script, improving error handling, adding example usage/documentation, and adding tests), the modification targets are the script itself, new tests under `tests/`, and new/updated docs under `docs/source/` with an `index.md` toctree update.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
fix jsonl_to_markdown

## PR Body
Fixes #162

Changes proposed in this pull request:
- Add `jsonl_to_markdown.py` utility script to convert JSONL files to Markdown format
- Script extracts the 'text' field from each line in a JSONL file and saves it as a separate Markdown file
- Include proper error handling for JSON decoding and file operations
- Add example usage and documentation

Checklist:
- Read contributing docs
- Updated docstrings
- Added tests

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
### MASCA (optimized for PR: `fix jsonl_to_markdown` / issue #162)

#### Mission
Ensure the `jsonl_to_markdown` utility works reliably as a supported conversion tool:
- Convert a JSONL file into per-record Markdown files by extracting the `text` field.
- Improve/solidify error handling (JSON decoding failures, missing fields, file I/O issues).
- Provide discoverable documentation and example usage.
- Add tests that lock in expected behavior.

#### App/Module Surfaces involved
- **Script entrypoint**: `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` (currently exists in repo and already implements basic functionality).
- **Docs**: Sphinx (MyST Markdown enabled). User-facing docs live under `docs/source/` and are navigated via `docs/source/index.md` toctrees.
- **Tests**: `tests/` has no existing coverage for this script; new tests need to be introduced.

#### Current Behavior (as-is)
- `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` defines `jsonl_to_markdown(input_file, output_dir)` and a minimal `__main__` CLI.
- For each input line (JSON object), it writes `output_dir/line_{N}.md` containing `data.get("text", "")`.
- Error handling is print-based:
  - On `json.JSONDecodeError`, prints an error and continues.
  - On other exceptions, prints an “Unexpected error” and continues.
  - No clear exit code semantics or summary of failures.

#### Gaps likely driving issue #162
- “Fix” suggests the existing script may have one or more of:
  - Incorrect CLI usage expectations (strict arg count, no `--help`, etc.).
  - Fragile error handling (swallows errors, continues silently producing empty files when `text` missing, etc.).
  - Unclear output naming or collisions.
  - Missing tests and missing docs discoverability.

#### Acceptance Targets (what to implement/change)
1. **Robust conversion function contract**
   - Decide desired behavior for:
     - Missing `text` field (skip? write empty file? error?).
     - Blank lines.
     - JSON decode failure (skip with counter; optionally fail fast).
   - Ensure output dir creation is reliable and errors are actionable.

2. **CLI improvements (if intended by issue/PR)**
   - Consider using `argparse` for:
     - `input_file`, `output_dir`
     - optional `--text-key` (default `text`)
     - optional `--prefix` or naming scheme
     - optional `--fail-fast` / `--skip-empty`
   - Ensure non-zero exit code when appropriate (e.g., cannot read input file, cannot write output, or all lines failed).

3. **Docs**
   - Add a new doc page under `docs/source/` documenting `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` usage and examples.
   - Link it from `docs/source/index.md` (new “Utilities/Scripts” toctree or add to an existing one).

4. **Tests**
   - Add tests that exercise:
     - happy path with multiple JSONL lines producing multiple `.md` files.
     - invalid JSON line handling.
     - missing `text` field behavior.
     - output directory creation.
   - Prefer calling the function directly (unit test) rather than spawning subprocess, unless CLI semantics are a key requirement.

#### Code hotspots / change points
- `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py::jsonl_to_markdown` (core conversion & error semantics).
- `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` `__main__` block (CLI contract and exit codes).
- New `tests/test_jsonl_to_markdown.py` (or similar).
- New docs page: `docs/source/jsonl_to_markdown.md` (or `docs/source/scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.md` if you add a `scripts/` docs folder).
- `docs/source/index.md` to add the page to navigation.

- tests/test_jsonl_to_markdown.py: No existing tests cover this script; PR checklist claims tests were added. Add unit tests to lock in behavior for valid/invalid JSONL lines, missing text field handling, and output file generation.
- docs/source/index.md: To make the new/updated utility discoverable in built docs, a new doc page should be linked in the Sphinx toctree.
- docs/source/jsonl_to_markdown.md: Add user-facing documentation and example usage for the script (MyST Markdown is enabled).

## Functions to Modify
- jsonl_to_markdown (scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py): Primary conversion logic; needs any bugfixes plus clarified behavior for missing `text`, invalid JSON, and file I/O errors, consistent with issue #162 and the PR description.
- __main__ (module entrypoint block) (scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py): CLI currently only checks positional arg count and prints usage. If issue #162 relates to usability or error behavior, adjust argument parsing and exit code handling here.

## Analysis Summary
Repository already contains `scripts/jsonl_to_markdown.py` implementing JSONL->multiple Markdown files by extracting the `text` field and writing `line_{N}.md`. There are currently no tests referencing this script and no docs page for it in `docs/source/`. To satisfy the PR description (fixing the script, improving error handling, adding example usage/documentation, and adding tests), the modification targets are the script itself, new tests under `tests/`, and new/updated docs under `docs/source/` with an `index.md` toctree update.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Harden JSONL→Markdown conversion logic: validate inputs, create output directory, skip blank lines, handle JSON decoding errors with counters, and define behavior for missing/empty `text` (e.g., skip vs write empty) while returning a structured summary (written/skipped/failed).
steps.reason: Issue #162 and PR description indicate current behavior is fragile (print-based errors, unclear semantics, potential empty files). A clear contract plus robust exception handling makes the utility reliable and testable.
steps.side_effects: May change output file count and naming compared to previous behavior (e.g., skipping records without `text`). Tests and docs must reflect the chosen contract; downstream users relying on empty-file outputs may need adjustment.

steps.operation: Replace the ad-hoc argv length check with an argparse-based CLI that supports `--help`, positional `input_file`/`output_dir`, optional flags (e.g., `--text-key`, `--prefix`, `--fail-fast`, `--skip-empty` as decided), and uses exit codes based on fatal vs non-fatal errors; print a concise end-of-run summary to stderr/stdout.
steps.reason: Current CLI is not discoverable and provides weak error semantics. Argparse improves usability and aligns with “proper error handling” and “example usage” requirements.
steps.side_effects: CLI interface/behavior may change (flags, output, exit codes). Any automation calling the script may need updates; add/adjust tests if CLI behavior is asserted.

step_plan.summary: Fix and solidify the existing `jsonl_to_markdown` utility by making conversion behavior explicit and resilient to malformed input and I/O failures, and by improving the CLI UX and exit-code semantics.
